workflow_name: Customer Health Score and Churn Risk Alert Pipeline
description: Weekday 6am Pacific pipeline that correlates Amplitude usage, Zendesk support, and Stripe billing signals to generate AI health scores for ~200 accounts, then routes critical/at-risk alerts to Slack channels, CSM emails, and executive stakeholders while suppressing healthy accounts. Accounts are processed sequentially via SplitInBatches (batchSize=1) with 300ms inter-account pacing.

trigger.description: Runs Monday-Friday at 6:00 AM Pacific (14:00 UTC)

steps.name: Schedule Trigger – Weekdays 6am Pacific
steps.description: Fires at 14:00 UTC Monday-Friday (6am Pacific). Entry point for the pipeline.
steps.error_handling: On failure, PagerDuty webhook fires via global error handler.

steps.name: Fetch Account List from CSM Directory API
steps.description: Fetches all active accounts with CSM email mappings, stripe_customer_id, and zendesk_org_id from the internal CSM directory API.

output_shape.accounts: array of {account_id, account_name, csm_email, tier, stripe_customer_id, zendesk_org_id}

steps.error_handling: Retry 3x; on persistent failure trigger PagerDuty and abort.

steps.name: Split Accounts Into Individual Items
steps.description: Splits the accounts array from the CSM API response into individual items for per-account processing. Preserves stripe_customer_id and zendesk_org_id for downstream filtering.
steps.error_handling: Throws if accounts array is empty; caught by global error handler.

steps.name: Loop Over Accounts One at a Time
steps.description: Processes accounts sequentially one at a time (batchSize=1). The 300ms pacing delay in the next step fires between each account iteration, preventing simultaneous API floods.
steps.error_handling: Any per-account error propagates to global handler; loop continues for remaining accounts.

steps.name: Rate-Limit Pacing Delay Between Accounts
steps.description: Inserts a 300ms delay between account processing iterations to avoid rate-limiting on Amplitude and Zendesk APIs. Passes account data through unchanged.
steps.error_handling: continueOnFail=true; any error propagates to global handler without aborting batch.

steps.name: Fetch Amplitude 30-Day Usage Metrics
steps.description: Calls Amplitude segmentation API scoped to the account's user segment for 30-day usage metrics including DAU and session counts.

output_shape.data: raw Amplitude segmentation response with event series

steps.error_handling: Retry 2x with 10s wait (respects Amplitude rate-limit window); on failure route via gate to inject amplitude_signals.available=false.

steps.name: Gate – Amplitude Fetch Success
steps.description: Checks if Amplitude HTTP call succeeded (non-error response). On failure branch, injects degraded amplitude_signals with available=false.
steps.error_handling: Routes to false branch on evaluation error.

steps.name: Inject Degraded Amplitude Signals on Failure
steps.description: Sets amplitude_signals.available=false so downstream steps can continue with partial data when Amplitude is unreachable. Logs a warning flag for ops visibility.

parameters.jsCode:
const item = $input.first().json;
return [{ json: Object.assign({}, item, { amplitude_signals: { available: false, dau_avg: 0, dau_trend: 'unknown', session_count_30d: 0, _degraded_reason: 'api_failure' } }) }];

steps.error_handling: continueOnFail=true; errors propagate to global handler.

steps.name: Parse Amplitude Usage Signals
steps.description: Parses Amplitude API response to extract DAU trend and session frequency signals for the past 30 days. Handles null gracefully.
steps.error_handling: continueOnFail=true; sets available=false on parse errors; continues with partial data.

steps.name: Fetch Zendesk 30-Day Support Tickets
steps.description: Queries Zendesk for tickets associated with the account over the past 30 days using zendesk_org_id (distinct from account_id) scoped search query. Fetches up to 100 results per page; pagination limited to first page with known-limitation flag.

output_shape.results: Zendesk search results with ticket array (up to 100 per page)
output_shape.next_page: pagination URL if more results exist

steps.error_handling: Retry 2x; on failure route via gate to inject zendesk_signals.available=false.

steps.name: Gate – Zendesk Fetch Success
steps.description: Checks if Zendesk HTTP call succeeded (results field present). On failure branch, injects degraded zendesk_signals with available=false.
steps.error_handling: Routes to false branch on evaluation error.

steps.name: Inject Degraded Zendesk Signals on Failure
steps.description: Sets zendesk_signals.available=false so downstream steps can continue with partial data when Zendesk is unreachable.

parameters.jsCode:
const item = $input.first().json;
return [{ json: Object.assign({}, item, { zendesk_signals: { available: false, ticket_count_30d: 0, escalated_count: 0, unresolved_count: 0, escalation_rate: 0, avg_resolution_hrs: 0, paginated_truncated: false } }) }];

steps.error_handling: continueOnFail=true; errors propagate to global handler.

steps.name: Parse Zendesk Support Health Signals
steps.description: Parses Zendesk response to compute ticket volume, escalation count, unresolved rate, and average resolution time. Sets zendesk_zero_tickets flag if no tickets found so LLM can account for data uncertainty. Sets paginated_truncated=true if next_page present.

zendesk_signals.zendesk_zero_tickets: boolean – true means no tickets found; may indicate org_id mismatch or genuinely no support load
zendesk_signals.paginated_truncated: boolean – true means >100 tickets exist; counts reflect first 100 only

steps.error_handling: continueOnFail=true; sets available=false on parse errors; continues with partial data.

steps.name: Check Stripe Customer ID Present
steps.description: Checks whether stripe_customer_id is non-empty. Sets has_stripe_customer_id boolean so the gate can route to degraded signals without making a malformed Stripe API call.
steps.error_handling: continueOnFail=true; defaults has_stripe_customer_id=false on error.

steps.name: Gate – Stripe Customer ID Present
steps.description: If stripe_customer_id is blank or missing, skip the Stripe API call entirely and inject degraded signals directly. Prevents malformed Stripe requests.
steps.error_handling: Routes to false branch (degraded) on evaluation error.

steps.name: Fetch Stripe Billing and Payment History
steps.description: Retrieves Stripe subscription status, MRR (via items.data[0].price), payment failures, and dunning flags for the specific customer (filtered by stripe_customer_id). Also expands latest invoice payment intent.

output_shape.data: Stripe subscription list filtered by customer with payment history

steps.error_handling: Retry 3x; on failure route via gate to inject stripe_signals.available=false.

steps.name: Gate – Stripe Fetch Success
steps.description: Checks if Stripe HTTP call succeeded (data field present). On failure branch, injects degraded stripe_signals with available=false.
steps.error_handling: Routes to false branch on evaluation error.

steps.name: Inject Degraded Stripe Signals on Failure
steps.description: Sets stripe_signals.available=false so downstream steps can continue with partial data when Stripe is unreachable or stripe_customer_id is missing.

parameters.jsCode:
const item = $input.first().json;
return [{ json: Object.assign({}, item, { stripe_signals: { available: false, mrr: 0, mrr_source: 'unavailable', subscription_status: 'unknown', payment_failures_detected: 0, payment_failures_note: 'data unavailable', in_dunning: false, cancel_at_period_end: false } }) }];

steps.error_handling: continueOnFail=true; errors propagate to global handler.

steps.name: Parse Stripe Billing Risk Signals
steps.description: Parses Stripe subscription data. MRR extracted via items.data[0].price.unit_amount (current Stripe API) with fallback to deprecated sub.plan.amount. payment_failures_detected is a proxy metric from latest invoice only; renamed from payment_failures_90d to accurately reflect scope. Includes a note field for LLM context.
steps.error_handling: continueOnFail=true; sets available=false on parse errors; continues with partial data.

steps.name: Validate Minimum Data Coverage (2-of-3 APIs)
steps.description: Checks that at least 2 of 3 data sources (Amplitude, Zendesk, Stripe) returned valid data. Outputs a boolean flag for gate routing.
steps.error_handling: continueOnFail=true; returns has_minimum_data=false if exception; gate routes to skip.

steps.name: Gate – Sufficient Data Coverage Check
steps.description: Routes accounts with at least 2 data sources available to LLM scoring; others are logged as skipped.
steps.error_handling: Routes to false branch on evaluation error.

steps.name: Log Skipped Account – Insufficient Data
steps.description: Records accounts skipped due to insufficient data coverage for audit purposes.
steps.error_handling: Log and continue; non-blocking.

steps.name: Assemble LLM Prompt with Account Signals
steps.description: Constructs the externalized LLM prompt by injecting account signals into the reviewable prompt template. Includes zendesk_zero_tickets and paginated_truncated flags so LLM can note data uncertainty. payment_failures_detected with scope note included. MRR=0 uncertainty noted when mrr_source=unavailable.

assignments.name: llm_prompt
assignments.value:
={{ 'You are a Customer Success analyst. Assess the health of the following B2B SaaS account and return a JSON object with exactly these fields: score (integer 0-100), headline (one sentence max 15 words summarizing health), suggestions (array of exactly 2-3 specific intervention strings tied to observed signals).

Account: ' + $json.account_name + ' (ID: ' + $json.account_id + ', Tier: ' + $json.tier + ')

Amplitude Usage (30 days):
- DAU average: ' + $json.amplitude_signals.dau_avg + '
- DAU trend: ' + $json.amplitude_signals.dau_trend + '
- Session count: ' + $json.amplitude_signals.session_count_30d + '
- Data available: ' + $json.amplitude_signals.available + '

Zendesk Support (30 days, up to 100 tickets per page):
- Ticket count: ' + $json.zendesk_signals.ticket_count_30d + '
- Escalated tickets: ' + $json.zendesk_signals.escalated_count + '
- Unresolved tickets: ' + $json.zendesk_signals.unresolved_count + '
- Escalation rate: ' + $json.zendesk_signals.escalation_rate + '%
- Avg resolution: ' + $json.zendesk_signals.avg_resolution_hrs + ' hrs
- Data available: ' + $json.zendesk_signals.available + '
- Zero tickets flag (may indicate org mapping issue, not necessarily healthy): ' + $json.zendesk_signals.zendesk_zero_tickets + '
- Results truncated at 100 (more pages exist): ' + $json.zendesk_signals.paginated_truncated + '

Stripe Billing:
- MRR: $' + $json.stripe_signals.mrr + ' (source: ' + $json.stripe_signals.mrr_source + '; if unavailable, do not treat $0 as healthy signal)
- Status: ' + $json.stripe_signals.subscription_status + '
- Payment failures detected: ' + $json.stripe_signals.payment_failures_detected + ' (' + $json.stripe_signals.payment_failures_note + ')
- In dunning: ' + $json.stripe_signals.in_dunning + '
- Cancel at period end: ' + $json.stripe_signals.cancel_at_period_end + '
- Data available: ' + $json.stripe_signals.available + '

Scoring guidance: 0-39 = critical churn risk, 40-69 = at-risk needs intervention, 70-100 = healthy. Weight usage decline, escalations, and payment failures most heavily. When data is unavailable or uncertain (noted above), do not treat absence as a positive signal. Return ONLY valid JSON, no markdown, no explanation.' }}

output_shape.llm_prompt: string – externalized reviewable prompt with injected signals including data-quality uncertainty flags

steps.error_handling: Expression errors throw; caught by global handler.

steps.name: LLM Health Score and Intervention Assessment
steps.description: Sends account signals to OpenAI GPT-4o to generate health score (0-100), one-sentence headline, and 2-3 specific signal-tied intervention suggestions.

messages.content: You are a B2B SaaS Customer Success analyst. Always respond with valid JSON only. No markdown, no explanation.

output_shape.choices: array with LLM message containing JSON score/headline/suggestions

steps.error_handling: Retry 2x; on persistent failure route via gate to skip log.

steps.name: Gate – LLM HTTP Call Succeeded
steps.description: Checks that the OpenAI HTTP call returned a choices array before attempting to parse. Routes failures directly to the LLM skip log.

output_shape.false_branch: log as LLM error and skip

steps.error_handling: Routes to false branch on evaluation error.

steps.name: Parse and Validate LLM Health Score Response
steps.description: Parses LLM JSON response, validates score is 0-100, headline is non-empty, suggestions is array of 2-3 items. Outputs llm_valid boolean for gate routing.
steps.error_handling: continueOnFail=true; sets llm_valid=false on any parse error; gate routes to skip.

steps.name: Gate – LLM Response Valid
steps.description: Routes accounts with valid LLM health scores to severity routing; invalid responses are logged and skipped.

output_shape.false_branch: account is logged as LLM error

steps.error_handling: Routes to false branch on evaluation error.

steps.name: Log Invalid LLM Response – Account Skipped
steps.description: Records accounts where LLM returned invalid or unparseable output for audit and retry review.
steps.error_handling: Log and continue; non-blocking.

steps.name: Determine Severity Bucket and Alert Flags
steps.description: Classifies account into critical (0-39), at_risk (40-69), or healthy (70-100) and sets boolean flags for alert routing. Healthy accounts suppressed.
steps.error_handling: continueOnFail=true; errors propagate to global handler.

steps.name: Check Alert Cooldown – Deduplication
steps.description: Reads n8n static workflow data to check if this account was alerted within the cooldown window (24h for critical, 48h for at-risk). Sets cooldown_suppressed boolean. Also writes last_alerted timestamp into static data when alert is allowed through.

output_shape.cooldown_suppressed: boolean – true if this account was already alerted within cooldown window
output_shape.last_alerted_ts: epoch ms of last alert, 0 if never

steps.error_handling: continueOnFail=true; on error, defaults cooldown_suppressed=false (allow alert through) to avoid missed critical alerts.

steps.name: Gate – Cooldown Suppressed
steps.description: If cooldown_suppressed is true, route to the cooldown log node and skip alert sending. Otherwise continue to should_alert gate.
steps.error_handling: Routes to false branch (allow through) on evaluation error to avoid missed critical alerts.

steps.name: Log Cooldown Suppressed Alert
steps.description: Records that this account's alert was suppressed due to cooldown deduplication.
steps.error_handling: Log and continue; non-blocking.

steps.name: Gate – Should Alert (Suppress Healthy)
steps.description: Suppresses healthy accounts from all alerts. Only critical and at-risk accounts proceed to notification steps.

output_shape.true_branch: critical or at-risk account proceeds to routing

steps.error_handling: Routes to false branch (suppress) on evaluation error.

steps.name: Suppress Healthy Account – No Alert
steps.description: Records healthy accounts as suppressed from alerting for audit trail.
steps.error_handling: Log and continue; non-blocking.

steps.name: Gate – Critical vs At-Risk Routing
steps.description: Routes critical accounts (0-39) to critical alert path with VP email; at-risk (40-69) to at-risk path with CSM only.
steps.error_handling: Routes to at-risk branch on evaluation error.

steps.name: Post Critical Alert to #cs-critical Slack Channel
steps.description: Posts a rich health score alert to the #cs-critical Slack channel with account name, score, headline, and intervention suggestions.
steps.error_handling: Retry 3x; log failure but continue to email step.

steps.name: Send Critical Alert Email to CSM and VP via SendGrid
steps.description: Sends critical health alert email to both CSM and CS VP using SendGrid template d-critical-health with dynamic account health data.
steps.error_handling: Retry 3x; log failure but continue pipeline.

steps.name: Mark Critical Account Alerts Sent
steps.description: Records alert outcome for the critical account for audit trail.
steps.error_handling: Log and continue.

steps.name: Post At-Risk Alert to #cs-at-risk Slack Channel
steps.description: Posts health score alert to #cs-at-risk Slack channel for accounts scoring 40-69 with headline and intervention suggestions.
steps.error_handling: Retry 3x; log failure but continue to CSM email.

steps.name: Send At-Risk Alert Email to CSM Only via SendGrid
steps.description: Sends at-risk health alert email to CSM only (no VP) using dedicated SendGrid template d-at-risk-health with dynamic account health data.
steps.error_handling: Retry 3x; log failure but continue pipeline.

steps.name: Mark At-Risk Account Alerts Sent
steps.description: Records alert outcome for the at-risk account for audit trail.
steps.error_handling: Log and continue.

steps.name: PagerDuty Failure Alert Webhook
steps.description: Fires PagerDuty Events API v2 webhook on workflow-level failure to page on-call engineer. Referenced by global error handler.

payload.summary: Customer Health Score Pipeline FAILED – churn alerts may not have sent

custom_details.workflow: Customer Health Score and Churn Risk Alert Pipeline

steps.error_handling: Best-effort; if PagerDuty also fails, error is logged in n8n execution history.

error_handling.on_workflow_failure: Trigger step_29 (PagerDuty webhook) via n8n error workflow configuration. PagerDuty is reserved for workflow-level failures (e.g., CSM API unreachable, schedule trigger failure) not per-account processing errors. All API retries configured per-step. Partial account failures are logged and do not abort the full run due to continueOnFail=true on all per-account Code nodes.
error_handling.runtime_notes: Sequential processing via SplitInBatches (batchSize=1) with 300ms inter-account delay. At average 5s total API latency per account (3 data APIs + LLM), 200 accounts = ~17 minutes. At p95 latency (15s/account), ~50 minutes. Worst-case (60s Amplitude + 30s Zendesk + 30s Stripe + 60s LLM timeouts) = 36,000s; global_timeout_seconds=7200 triggers PagerDuty in that scenario. Recommended: increase to batchSize=5 with 1500ms inter-batch delay if runtime pressure observed. Alert deduplication uses n8n static workflow data – this persists across runs within the same workflow instance but resets on workflow redeploy.
error_handling.continueOnFail_note: All Code nodes within the per-account loop (steps 4, 5_fail, 6, 7_fail, 8, 9_guard, 9_fail, 10, 11, 14, 16, 19, 19b) have continueOnFail=true set explicitly. This ensures a single account anomaly never aborts the full 200-account batch.

output.destination: Slack (#cs-critical, #cs-at-risk), SendGrid email (CSM + VP for critical via d-critical-health template; CSM only for at-risk via d-at-risk-health template), n8n execution log for all accounts
output.format: Slack rich text message + SendGrid templated HTML email + JSON audit records per account
output.description: Per-account health scores 0-100 with one-sentence headline and 2-3 signal-tied intervention suggestions routed by severity. Healthy accounts suppressed. Duplicate alerts suppressed by cooldown (24h critical, 48h at-risk) via static workflow data. All outcomes logged.

credentials_needed: SendGrid API key (Bearer token) – requires both d-critical-health and d-at-risk-health templates
Slack incoming webhook URLs for #cs-critical and #cs-at-risk
PagerDuty Events API v2 integration key
Internal CSM Directory API auth token (must return stripe_customer_id and zendesk_org_id per account)
security.pii_handling: Account names, CSM emails, and MRR figures processed in-memory only. No PII written to persistent storage within the workflow. Restrict n8n instance access to limit execution log exposure. n8n static workflow data (alert cooldown log) stored in n8n's internal database – ensure instance is secured.
security.data_flow_notes: LLM prompt contains account business metrics only, not end-user PII. Ensure OpenAI data processing addendum is in place. All credentials stored in n8n credential vault. Zendesk query uses zendesk_org_id (not account_id) to avoid silent org-mapping mismatches. Stripe MRR uses items.data[0].price.unit_amount with fallback to deprecated plan.amount.

cost_estimate.llm_inference: ~200 accounts x ~1100 tokens/call = ~220K tokens/run. At GPT-4o pricing (~$5/1M input, ~$15/1M output), estimated $1.25-$2.75/run
cost_estimate.api_calls: 200 x 3 data APIs = 600 HTTP calls + 200 CSM lookups + up to 200 Slack posts + up to 200 SendGrid sends
cost_estimate.n8n_credits: ~1400-1800 node executions/run depending on skip rates (higher due to added guard/cooldown/gate nodes)
cost_estimate.total_estimate: $5-$15/run typical; $20 upper bound on high-alert days

test_cases.name: Happy path – critical account triggers all alerts including VP email
At-risk account – Slack to #cs-at-risk and CSM email only, no VP, uses d-at-risk-health template
Healthy account – suppressed, no Slack or email alerts sent
Insufficient data – only 1 of 3 APIs available, account skipped before LLM
LLM returns unparseable response – account skipped gracefully with audit log

input.mock_llm_response_override: Sorry I cannot help with that.

test_cases.name: Amplitude API fails – degraded signals injected, pipeline continues with Zendesk+Stripe
Stripe missing stripe_customer_id – Stripe fetch skipped entirely via guard gate, signals degrade gracefully
LLM HTTP call fails (no choices array) – routed directly to LLM skip log via step_15_check gate
Alert cooldown – critical account alerted yesterday, second run suppresses duplicate
Zendesk zero tickets flag – zero tickets sets zendesk_zero_tickets=true for LLM uncertainty awareness
Stripe MRR falls back to deprecated plan.amount when items.data not present
Zendesk result set >100 tickets – paginated_truncated flag set true
